meta._ui: Illustrative sample of the v2 (MINIMAL + CAPABILITIES) contract. The items below between them exercise EVERY object in export-schema-v2.ts. Some refs (alterations/accessories/companions sku codes, pill targets) point at items outside this small slice; in a real export every referenced sku exists in items[]. Any key starting with `_ui` is plain-English DOCUMENTATION ONLY — the backend MUST strip every `_ui` key on ingest; `_ui` is NOT part of the schema. counts here describe THIS sample, not the full catalog.
meta.source: leicht_units v781 (headless DOM extraction via openDetail) — SAMPLE
meta.note: Minimal + capabilities export of v781. Each item is the THIN shape: capabilities REPLACES programmeAvailability (raw u.x excludedPrograms + tier/depth/handle/open/antoso/door gate inputs, isFrmatFamily kept so the backend layers the FRMAT max-size rule); configure→parameters (label+sku pills only); accessoryPanel→alterations/accessories(+variants)/finishInterior; relatedGroups→companions; engineering→{key,ok}. Built by driving the app's own openDetail()/visibleBlocks()/famInTaskSub(); 23 app-deleted artifact units recovered from the raw <script id="DATA"> (see meta.recoveredArtifactSkus); 21 referenced non-unit codes synthesized as minimal accessory/part/alteration stubs.

categories._ui: LEFT SIDEBAR TYPE taxonomy (distinct from functionalCategories). Trimmed: only 3 of the real 14 categories, and Base keeps only the sub-categories this sample's items live in. On each item: category/subcategory/section.
categories.name: Base

programmes._ui: TOP-LEFT dropdown kitchen lines. Items refer to these ids in Capabilities.excludedPrograms. 6 shown (real export = 120), covering PRIMO/AVANCE/CONTINO/CONTINO-12. "273" (VERVE-FS) and "561" (CARRÉ-FS) appear in TK7080BZ2.capabilities.excludedPrograms above — so that item greys in exactly those programmes.
programmes.name: ROCCA 01

ruleTables._ui: Passthrough reference tables (handle/opening/antoso/lighting/…). Trimmed to 2 of the real 10 for readability — the backend passes the whole object through unchanged (RuleTables = Record<string, unknown>). Omit entirely or send {} if unused.

metal_bow.label: Metal Bow Handle
metal_bow.note: Standard handle option, free with cabinet

bar.label: Bar Handle
bar.note: Length by door/drawer width; extra charge

P1.label: P1 — Primo, one handle on top
P1.note: One handle on top; the bottom drawers open via 760 (touch) or 761 (electric). Primo programmes; only on P1-marked units.

C1.label: C1 — Contino, one top channel
C1.note: One Contino channel on top; the bottom drawers open via 760 (touch) or 761 (electric).

systems._ui: SYSTEM BUILDER bundle. Attaches to an item's detail screen when its sku ∈ triggerSkus. required[]/optional[] are SystemSlots; each slot lists SlotOption skus with an optional default (e.g. the 100/160/200 cm connecting-cable pick). Only the runtime clipboard + status ticks are UI-only; this COMPOSITION is exported.
systems.name: SensoMatic System
LLE-R Recessed Light System
systems.note: Recessed LED lamp — 7.8 cm drill hole; position decides the drilling type.

functionalCategories._ui: The "Design Tasks" SECOND sidebar (render-ready; distinct from categories[]). Trimmed to show shape: inspiration + allCategories + ONE zone (Base) with ONE group (Water) and 2 of its leaves + ONE moreCategory (Alteration, 2 subs). A leaf claims an item when ANY of its match[] rules hits the item's cat/sub/section/familyId; membership is materialized per item in Item.functionalGroups[]. Real export has 4 zones + 7 moreCategories.

items._ui: RICH CABINET — one orderable code + its detail screen. Exercises Capabilities (the pill-gate rule inputs), Parameters (W/H/D/Programme pills incl. P1/C1 opening), thin ref lists (alterations = sku codes; accessories = a mix of plain sku strings AND {sku,variants} for the L3/M3·M8 runner + 1m/1.6m/2m cable rows), companions, finishes (trimmed to 3), toeKick, and one functionalGroups leaf. Note accessories[] here shows BOTH AccessoryRef shapes.
items.name: Cooktop Unit · Top Blender · BZ2

capabilities._ui: ⭐ THE RULE INPUTS. The client feeds this + its toolbar to availableFromCaps() (see schema) to decide if a configure PILL that TARGETS this sku is live/grey/dead. Here: nativeTier "P" native + twinTiers ["A"] → greys under an AVANCE-only toolbar; excludedPrograms ["273","280","281","295","561"] → greyed in those 5 programmes (273=VERVE-FS, 561=CARRÉ-FS, both in this sample's programmes[]); openP1:true so the P1 opening pill resolves; depthClasses [58,68].

restrictions: Cupboards 45 degree chamfered edge on both sides
planningNotes: Control panel on top.

parameters._ui: The H/W/D/Programme pill rows (card + Configure box). THIN in v2 — each pill is just {label, sku}; availability + selected state are DERIVED at read (selected = pill.sku===item.sku; grey = availableFromCaps(target.caps)===false; dead = sku null/absent). The "63" depth pill carries alteration:true; programme pills P1/C1 carry opening:true.

modifications.title: P1 — Primo, one handle on top
modifications.text: order P1TK7080BZ2. Bottom drawers open 760 (touch) or 761 (electric).

modifications.title: Handle-less mechanical (760)
modifications.text: Tip-Softclose touch latch. eOpus: set handle 760

modifications.title: Handle-less electric (761)
modifications.warn: 761 electric ALWAYS needs the SMNT transformer — not added automatically

didYouKnow.text: This cabinet can also be ordered 63 / 68 cm deep — same front, deeper carcase. 63 cm = the 68 cm cabinet with a factory depth alteration.

items._ui: VERO FRONT — the only kind with finishInterior. Exercises FinishInterior/Swatch/VisibleSideCombo, DidYouKnow, and the engineering[] {key,ok} flags.
items.name: Vero-glass floor unit · With Lights

description.title: Vero-glass floor unit
bullets: 1 glass door with anodised aluminium frame and integrated handle
2 vertical, dynamic lights for cupboard sides
Light temperature 2700K or variable of 2700 to 5000K with dimmer possible

planningNotes: Cupboard interior colour = cupboard exterior colour.
Please indicate colour of the frame on the order!
Change of handle or glass not possible.

finishInterior._ui: The Vero "Finish interior" sub-panel (NOT sku refs). swatches[] = the colour-square grid (trimmed 19→3; img built from the Finish host); visibleSideCombos[] = the "which visible-side finishes are allowed per interior finish" table (catalog ch.11, trimmed →3); optionCodes[] = extra-charge interior-style CHIPS — plain codes (MPK/KH/KG …), do NOT make cards/lookups from them.

didYouKnow._ui: The 💡 "Did you know?" cross-order tip. codes[] each carry a sibling sku the tip links to.
didYouKnow.text: This cabinet can also be ordered 36 cm deep — same front, deeper carcase.

items._ui: DOOR CABINET — carries handedLR:true (the "L/R" left/right-hinge badge), modifications[] ("Modifications — how to": 760/761 handle-less recipes), didYouKnow, and accessories[] with the IGS2058 {sku,variants:[{label:"L3/M3"},{label:"M8"}]} pair. Exercises Modification + handedLR + the variant AccessoryRef.
items.name: Floor unit
restrictions: Cupboards 45 degree chamfered edge not on both sides

parameters._ui: accessories below carry the IGS2058 L3/M3·M8 variant pair — the canonical {sku,variants} AccessoryRef.

items._ui: TRASH/SINK PULLOUT (Base · Sinks) — exercises sinkFitment AND nameQualifier ("Trash Pullout", the amber sub-label after the title, from vsub[vr]). Also a good second handedLR/engineering example.
items.name: Instant Hot Sink Blender — 30 cm door

description.title: Sink waste bin unit for hot
bullets: 1 door 30 cm wide in front of compartment for taps accessories
1 frame with 3 waste bins, contents 1x 14 ltr., 1x 10 ltr. and 1x 10 ltr. with bio lid, in addition 2x 2.5 ltr.

planningNotes: The compartment for taps accessories determines style left or right.
The true width for the installation of boilers and cartridges is 26.7 cm.
Please plan sink installation above pullout, available height 28 cm to carcase top edge!

sinkFitment._ui: The Base/Sinks "Max Sink Size: NN″" line + "＋ Add Sink" popup + detail "Sink fitment" section. showOnCard:true drives the card ＋Add-Sink button; maxSinkSizeInch/cabinetWidthCm come from the width; customAboveInch:42 is the "needs a custom unit" cutoff.
notes: Cabinet width 80 cm → fits sink bowls up to 30″.
Sinks over 42″ (or wider than 120 cm base) require a custom sink unit.
Drawer/pullout sink — no hinge modification (ANSVVO275 does not apply).

modifications.title: Handle-less mechanical (760)
modifications.text: Tip-Softclose touch latch. eOpus: set handle 760

items._ui: REFRIGERATOR appliance front. Exercises ApplianceHousing (Refrigerators variant), the E-mode capability path (hasEFront/onePieceFront/excludedProgramsE), handleFree/singleHandle, InspirationBlock, frontModifiers (spec "Modifiers" line), and a dead alteration DimPill.
items.name: Appliance door for large Fridge/Freezer

capabilities._ui: E-MODE example. hasEFront:true + onePieceFront:true (one-piece front) + a NON-empty excludedProgramsE[] — those extra exclusions apply ONLY when the toolbar is in single-front / "Full-E" mode (state.front===1). handleFree:true + singleHandle:true (no-handle appliance door). excludedPrograms/excludedProgramsE trimmed to 8 each (real, but the full lists are 27/10).

description.title: Appliance door for large capacity appliance
bullets: Handle and hinge drill holes on site

planningNotes: Also available for SUB-ZERO 700 series, Miele and B/S/H appliances.
Please state appliance manufacturer and appliance type.

appliance._ui: APPLIANCES popup (card button, tooltip "Appliances"). Refrigerators shape = brand·category·nicheSize ONLY (no subcategory/note — those are Dishwashers-only). category "Refrigerators" picks the fridge glyph; nicheSize "18"" derives from width 460 mm.

parameters._ui: Note the depth "63" pill: {label:"63", alteration:true} with NO sku → a DEAD pill (renders grey + inert, nowhere to navigate).

inspiration._ui: The camera-icon lightbox photo (top-level card mirror so the list API returns it). heading = code/dims line; imageUrl on the inspiration host; fullScreen → "View full screen".
inspiration.caption: Example of a fully integrated dishwasher front. Actual finish depends on the selected programme and door style.
inspiration.heading: GF46204 — Appliance door for large Fridge/Freezer · W 460 × H 2039 × D 20 mm

items._ui: DISHWASHER FRONT (Base · Appliance housing) — the DW ApplianceHousing shape (subcategory + note), plus faceForTiers ["_","C"] (the tier contexts in which this unit is its family's FACE card — NOT derivable, captured from the app).

capabilities._ui: opening:"P1" — THIS unit IS the premium P1 opening variant ("one handle on top"). availableTiers is ["P1"], so under any tier ≠ P1 the tierOk gate fails. (P1/C1 codes are a prefix on a base; most are synthesized, but recovered/real ones like this are stored.)

restrictions: Cupboards 45 degree chamfered edge not on both sides
planningNotes: If a rear panel is required for a sink unit, please always order this unit with the code MP RU.

notes: Cabinet width 45 cm → fits sink bowls up to 12″.
Sinks over 42″ (or wider than 120 cm base) require a custom sink unit.
Deep-basin door rule: if basin depth exceeds 8″ (203 mm), add ANSVVO275 (top hinge dropped 27.5 cm for clearance). Not needed for basins ≤ 8″.

restrictions: Cupboards 45 degree chamfered edge on both sides

This type is intended for planning to use finishing tops or further cupboards.

parameters._ui: options[] = the flattened coded selector row(s) beyond W/H/D/Programme — here "Ty" (S7 · Z · SZ). Each OptionPill is {group,label,sku}; clicking opens that sibling. (swatch is set only on the handle "Finish" row — none in this sample; 0 in the real export.)

capabilities._ui: isFrmatFamily:true — the 1-unit FRMAT max-size-table family. The backend layers FRMAT_MAX[programmeName] ON TOP of the normal gate (the sole residual in the 99.997% capabilities verification). Also shows faceForTiers ["_","P","A","C"] (face in every tier context).

description.title: Front panel material
bullets: Bevelled edges not possible! thickness and reverse side décor correspond with the respective front finish

planningNotes: Please state length and width.
Texture direction = length Ordered size of less than 0.4 m² are calculated on the price of 0.4 m².

items._ui: RECOVERED ARTIFACT (LED control) — from meta.recoveredArtifactSkus. Exercises Capabilities.alwaysAvailable:true + nativeTier:null.
items.name: LED dynamic light control

capabilities._ui: alwaysAvailable:true (app u._c) short-circuits EVERY gate → the pill never greys. nativeTier:null = line-neutral (never greys by tier). This is one of meta.recoveredArtifactSkus — a code the app's init deleted as an artifact but which is a real orderable unit, recovered from the raw <script id="DATA">.

description.title: LED dynamic light control controller set DALI
bullets: 1 DALI controller with 9-way distributor for connection to power supply unit L24 NT ...

planningNotes: Control using home control with DALI DT8 protocol.
Integration in KNX systems possible with customer's KNX module for DALI DT8 control.

items._ui: ALTERATION item (kind:"alteration") — the target of every cabinet's alterations[] sku list (e.g. TK7080BZ2 above lists "ANST"). One code, referenced by thousands of units, stored ONCE.
items.name: Cupboard alteration · one dimension
planningNotes: Extra charge = cupboard reduction / front reduction Base type is the next bigger order unit.

items._ui: ACCESSORY item (kind:"accessory") — a plinth cover. The target of an accessories[] / companions[] ref, stored once. Also carries faceForTiers. (kind "part" has ZERO instances in v781 — the fourth ItemKind value is valid but unused here.)

items._ui: Line-66 + tall-extension example. doorLineYCode = the ORDER CODE when the toolbar picks door-line Y: Y REPLACES the whole code (V/E/J/P1/C1 are only prefixes/suffixes), so it cannot be derived and ships literally; capabilities.doorLineY is the matching GATE. heightExtension = the app's "217+" chip appended to the Height row — picking 230/244/250 cm opens the family's 217 cm unit and orders addCode (MPHVERL) alongside it, the height twin of the 63 cm depth alteration. It is NOT part of parameters.height because some families also have real 230/250 cm siblings and the labels would collide.
